The Objective Residue Classification Oracle (ORCO) computes the posterior probability of each amino acid class given a set of backbone chemical shifts observed in NMR. Observed shifts are compared against per-residue, per-secondary-structure reference means and standard deviations from the Wishart RefDB using a naive-Gaussian likelihood, with secondary-structure states marginalized out. The reference uses the six backbone atoms HA, CA, CB, CO, N, and HN.

Columns: TopP = top posterior; Margin = TopP - second posterior; Entropy = posterior entropy in nats; Ch = observed channels.

Please cite the following: Manuscript in process.
Reference data: Zhang H, Neal S, Wishart DS. RefDB: A database of uniformly referenced protein chemical shifts. J. Biomol. NMR 25:173–195 (2003). Per-residue, per-secondary-structure means and standard deviations taken from https://refdb.wishartlab.com/stat.html. Proline amide N (μ = 135.627, σ = 5.150, N = 2819) is back-filled identically across coil/helix/beta states because RefDB does not tabulate a per-secondary-structure proline amide N; this is the only modification.

Input format (SHIFTY):

Header: #NUM AA HA CA CB CO N HN. Use 0.00 for missing shifts. The AA field is passed through and not used for classification.

#NUM AA  HA    CA    CB    CO     N      HN
1    X   4.05  54.91 32.83 172.14 0.00   0.00
2    ?   4.64  54.42 41.39 175.10 0.00   0.00
3    R   4.33  55.97 30.99 174.82 121.91 8.36
4    V   4.31  60.10 34.32 176.08 118.71 7.99

Paste chemical shifts in SHIFTY format below. The example shown is ubiquitin (BMRB 6457).

ORCO result preview (top 3 per residue shown below; full top-20 table available via download)
